tipRules.tip: KVD: 'Always have a Sexy Dawg topwater tied on in summer. No matter how hot, there are always shallow bass willing to hit topwater at sunrise or sunset. Use 40lb braid — helps it walk better on surface AND gives firmer hooksets at end of long casts vs mono. 7-4 rod with soft forgiving tip to keep fish hooked.'

KVD: 'As summer day heats up and topwater fades, tie on a 6XD to dig main lake points and deep vegetation to 20ft on 12lb line. Clear water = Blue Gizzard Shad color. Dingy = Sexy Shad. Long 7-10 rod to launch as far as possible — longer cast maximizes diving depth. Slow 5.3:1 reel for deep cranking torque.'
